channel.title: Technology - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Valve's Steam Machine ships June 29 for $1,049, but you probably won't be able to buy one yet - Ars Technica
item.description:

item.title: Following user outcry, AMD reinstates memory encryption in consumer CPUs - Ars Technica
item.description:
1. Following user outcry, AMD reinstates memory encryption in consumer CPUs  Ars Technica
  2. AMD silently removes memory encryption from consumer Ryzen CPUs, leaving users unaware that they may be vulnerable — security feature vanishes after newer AGESA firmware, AMD engineers go radio silent when pressed about the change  Tom's Hardware
  3. AMD to Restore TSME (Memory Encryption) on Consumer Ryzen Processors After Backlash  TechPowerUp
  4. AMD Reverses Course On Removing TSME From Ryzen Chips; Will Reinstate The Feature Through A New BIOS Update  Wccftech
  5. AMD to restore TSME support to Ryzen 9000 CPUs after backlash  OC3D
